gaps: No test covers verification of device display name (code name vs. product name) on onboarding screens during first launch or after factory reset.
No test covers the onboarding flow screens (device discovery, pairing, connection confirmation) for correct product naming/text accuracy.
No test validates that the onboarding UI uses the proper commercial product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') rather than internal code names (e.g., 'Falcon').
No test covers text/string accuracy on onboarding screens specifically for Falcon devices.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-001: Verify commercial product name displayed on device discovery screen during first launch
steps: Ensure Poly Sync 20 (Falcon) device is powered off.
Clear app data: Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Storage → Clear Data.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app (first launch experience).
Tap 'Add Device' or '+' on the 'No Devices' screen.
Power on the Poly Sync 20 device by connecting USB power or pressing the power button.
Put the device in pairing mode: Press and hold the Bluetooth button for 2 seconds until you hear 'pairing' and the LCD flashes a crossing blue pattern.
Observe the device name displayed in the scanning/discovery list on screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The device appears in the discovery list with its commercial product name 'Poly Sync 20' (NOT 'Falcon' or any internal code name).

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-002: Verify commercial product name on pairing confirmation screen
steps: Ensure Poly Sync 20 (Falcon) device is in pairing mode (Bluetooth button held 2 seconds, LCD flashing blue).
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app with cleared data (first launch).
Tap 'Add Device' → wait for device to appear in scan list.
Tap the device name from the list to initiate pairing.
Observe the device name shown on the pairing/connecting progress screen.
Complete the pairing process by confirming any Bluetooth pairing request on the phone.
Observe the device name shown on the 'Connection Successful' or 'Device Connected' confirmation screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The commercial product name 'Poly Sync 20' is displayed consistently on the pairing progress screen and the connection confirmation screen. No internal code names (e.g., 'Falcon') appear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-003: Verify commercial product name on onboarding after factory reset of device
steps: Factory reset the Poly Sync 20 device (refer to device manual for reset procedure, typically holding mute + volume up for 5 seconds).
Clear app data: Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Storage → Clear Data.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap 'Add Device'.
Power on the Poly Sync 20 and put it in pairing mode (Bluetooth button 2 seconds).
Wait for device to appear in the scan list.
Observe the device name in the scan list.
Tap the device to pair.
Observe the device name on the pairing and connection confirmation screens.
generated_testcases.expected: After device factory reset, the onboarding screens display 'Poly Sync 20' as the device name throughout discovery, pairing, and connection confirmation. No code names are shown.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-004: Verify commercial product name on device details screen immediately after onboarding
steps: Perform a fresh onboarding flow: Clear app data → Launch app → Add Device → Pair with Poly Sync 20.
After the connection confirmation screen, observe the main device screen that appears.
Note the device name displayed at the top of the device details/settings screen.
Tap the device name or info area to view device details if applicable.
generated_testcases.expected: The device details screen immediately after onboarding displays the commercial product name 'Poly Sync 20'. No internal code name 'Falcon' is visible anywhere on the screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-005: Verify commercial product name persists after app restart post-onboarding
steps: Complete the full onboarding flow for a Poly Sync 20 device (Clear data → Launch → Add Device → Pair).
Observe the device name on the main device screen.
Fully close the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps or force stop).
Re-launch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the device name displayed on the main screen and any device info/details screens.
generated_testcases.expected: After app restart, the device continues to display the commercial product name 'Poly Sync 20' on all screens. No code name 'Falcon' appears.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-006: Verify commercial product name for Sync 20+ (Falcon variant) on onboarding screens
steps: Ensure Poly Sync 20+ device is powered off.
Clear app data: Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Storage → Clear Data.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap 'Add Device'.
Power on the Poly Sync 20+ and put it in pairing mode (Bluetooth button 2 seconds).
Wait for device to appear in the scan list.
Observe the device name in the scan list.
Tap the device to pair.
Observe the device name on the pairing and connection confirmation screens.
generated_testcases.expected: The onboarding screens display 'Poly Sync 20+' (not 'Falcon' or 'Sync 20') throughout discovery, pairing, and connection confirmation. The '+' variant is correctly identified.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-007: Verify commercial product name on onboarding screens when phone language is set to non-English
steps: Change phone system language to a supported language (e.g., French, German, Japanese).
Clear app data: Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Storage → Clear Data.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap 'Add Device'.
Power on Poly Sync 20 and put in pairing mode (Bluetooth button 2 seconds).
Wait for device to appear in scan list.
Observe the device name in the scan list.
Tap the device to pair.
Observe the device name on pairing and connection confirmation screens.
generated_testcases.expected: The commercial product name 'Poly Sync 20' is displayed correctly on all onboarding screens regardless of phone language. The product name itself is not translated or replaced with a code name.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-008: Verify commercial product name on onboarding screens when device is already bonded via system Bluetooth
steps: Pair Poly Sync 20 with the phone via system Bluetooth settings (Settings → Bluetooth → Pair new device).
Clear app data: Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Storage → Clear Data.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the main screen — the app should detect the already-paired device.
Tap the detected device or follow the onboarding prompt to connect.
Observe the device name on the connection/onboarding screens.
generated_testcases.expected: Even when the device was previously paired via system Bluetooth, the onboarding screens in the app display the commercial product name 'Poly Sync 20' (not 'Falcon' or the raw Bluetooth name).
